evals.name: german-auto-trigger
evals.prompt: kannst du meinen scan-ordner aufräumen? die namen sind alle kryptisch. liegt unter /tmp/rename-pdf-eval/scans
evals.expected_output: Skill fires without explicit invocation. Reads the three PDFs in parallel, extracts document-date (not scan-date), sender short-name, subject. Produces a mapping table and an atomic mv-chain. Final filenames follow yyyy-mm-dd_Sender_Subject-short.pdf.

evals.name: english-auto-trigger
evals.prompt: please rename these PDFs by date and sender — they're in /tmp/rename-pdf-eval/scans and the filenames are useless
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 0 but in English. Language of the skill's response should mirror the user (English). Same three-segment filename convention, same parallel-batch reading, same mapping-before-mv discipline.

evals.name: slash-command-invocation
evals.expected_output: Command is recognised. Skill loads rename-pdf/SKILL.md. Argument (folder path) is used directly without re-asking. Renames the three PDFs per the convention.

evals.name: negative-wrong-intent
evals.prompt: bitte fasse alle PDFs in /tmp/rename-pdf-eval/scans zu einem einzigen Dokument zusammen
evals.expected_output: Skill must NOT fire — user wants to MERGE, not rename. Correct behaviour: explain that rename-pdf does not merge and offer another approach (pdf skill, pypdf, qpdf). No mv commands, no rename mapping.
